title: rubus frondifer
intro: Rubus frondifer, also known as the thimbleberry or western thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers and edible red fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus frondifer include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Rubus frondifer can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its fruit can also be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves.
